entry.title: DéPOT 2026: Worker Health and Pollution II

media:group.media:title: DéPOT 2026: Worker Health and Pollution II

entry.title: DéPOT 2026: Environmental Pasts and Futures in Mining II

media:group.media:title: DéPOT 2026: Environmental Pasts and Futures in Mining II
media:group.media:description:
Deindustrialization and the Environment in Post-Socialist Europe
with Ondřej Ševeček and Vítězslav Sommer, Nina Vodopivec, Emese Dobos,
Juliette Ronsin and Loman-Pierre Charrier and Mathis Gronau.
What can the study of deindustrialization add to our understanding of environmental history? And conversely – what can the study of environmental history contribute to deindustrialization studies? Answering these questions will be the focus of the 2026 Deindustrialization and the Politics of Our Time (DePOT) annual conference, whose theme is “Deindustrialization and the Environment.”

media:group.media:title: DéPOT 2026: Dirty Old Town (Expo Tour)
media:group.media:description:
Deindustrialization and the Environment in Post-Socialist Europe
with Ondřej Ševeček and Vítězslav Sommer, Nina Vodopivec, Emese Dobos,
Juliette Ronsin and Loman-Pierre Charrier and Mathis Gronau.
What can the study of deindustrialization add to our understanding of environmental history? And conversely – what can the study of environmental history contribute to deindustrialization studies? Answering these questions will be the focus of the 2026 Deindustrialization and the Politics of Our Time (DePOT) annual conference, whose theme is “Deindustrialization and the Environment.”

media:group.media:description:
Deindustrialization and the Environment in Post-Socialist Europe
with Ondřej Ševeček and Vítězslav Sommer, Nina Vodopivec, Emese Dobos,
Juliette Ronsin and Loman-Pierre Charrier and Mathis Gronau.
What can the study of deindustrialization add to our understanding of environmental history? And conversely – what can the study of environmental history contribute to deindustrialization studies? Answering these questions will be the focus of the 2026 Deindustrialization and the Politics of Our Time (DePOT) annual conference, whose theme is “Deindustrialization and the Environment.”

entry.title: DéPOT 2026: Deindustrialization and the Environment in Post-Socialist Europe

media:group.media:title: DéPOT 2026: Deindustrialization and the Environment in Post-Socialist Europe
media:group.media:description:
Deindustrialization and the Environment in Post-Socialist Europe
with Ondřej Ševeček and Vítězslav Sommer, Nina Vodopivec, Emese Dobos,
Juliette Ronsin and Loman-Pierre Charrier and Mathis Gronau.
What can the study of deindustrialization add to our understanding of environmental history? And conversely – what can the study of environmental history contribute to deindustrialization studies? Answering these questions will be the focus of the 2026 Deindustrialization and the Politics of Our Time (DePOT) annual conference, whose theme is “Deindustrialization and the Environment.”

entry.title: DéPOT 2026: Environmental Pasts and Futures in Mining I

media:group.media:title: DéPOT 2026: Environmental Pasts and Futures in Mining I
media:group.media:description: with Turner Adornetto, Montaine Pichot, Sarah MacDonell, and Pascal Raggi

media:group.media:title: DéPOT 2026: A Collective Book Launch
media:group.media:description:
Join us for the collective book event celebrating recent publications produced by the Deindustrialization and the Politics of Our Time (DePOT) research project!
Intro 4th Space Opening Remarks by Steven High Steven High and Stefan Berger, eds. _The Politics of Industrial Closure: Transnational Patterns and Comparisons_ Pascal Raggi et Fanny Gallot, dir. _Ouvriers et ouvrières dans la désindustrialisation._ Indranil Chakraborty and Steven High, eds. _Deindustrialization and Economic Restructuring in Post-Reform India._ Cory Haala, _When Democrats Won the Heartland: Progressive Populism in the Age of Reagan, 1978-1992_ John Sandlos and Arn Keeling, _The Price of Gold: Mining, Pollution and Resistance in Yellowknife._ (McGill Queen's University Press). Tom Fraser, Invested in Crisis: _Public Sector Pensions Against the Future_
This is part of the (Un)just Transitions? Deindustrialization and the Environment conference that looks to explore these and other aspects of the intersection between deindustrialization and the environment.

entry.title: Agents in the Field: Agentic AI for Climate Science and Ecologywith Genevieve Patterson

media:group.media:title: Agents in the Field: Agentic AI for Climate Science and Ecologywith Genevieve Patterson
media:group.media:description:
Join us for the first part of the Leadership in Environmental and Digital innovation for Sustainability (LEADS) Wrap-up Event.
Agentic AI systems can now plan, write, and execute scientific workflows with limited human intervention. This talk shows how familiar tasks for ecologists, biologists, and climate scientists can be reframed as modular, agent-driven pipelines. I demonstrate recently popular approaches to ecological case studies from my own work, including macaque behavior tracking and green crab molting detection. The focus is on practical capabilities, limitations, and how to keep these systems scientifically reliable.
AI systems can now plan, write, and execute scientific workflows with limited human intervention.
Part of LEADS Wraps Up!
LEADS is Leadership in Environmental and Digital innovation for Sustainability (LEADS) is an NSERC-CREATE graduate program on the theme of science leadership for global sustainability in the digital age.

entry.title: Plurilinguisme en Éducation 2026: Projection et discussion avec Mama Adobea Nii-OwooNo

media:group.media:title: Plurilinguisme en Éducation 2026: Projection et discussion avec Mama Adobea Nii-OwooNo
media:group.media:description:
Rejoignez-nous pour le dernier événement de Pluri-Ed-J 2 : Plurilinguisme en action – perspectives, ressources et pratiques!
Projection et discussion du documentaire en anglais "No Vernacular! - A Journey Through Ghana’s Multilingual Classrooms" par Mama Adobea Nii-OwooNo ("Vernacular! - Un voyage au cœur des classes plurilingues au Ghana).
PLURI-ED-J-2 mettra de l’avant des modèles issus de la recherche, des ressources didactiques et pédagogiques, des ateliers ainsi que des pistes concrètes pour mettre le plurilinguisme en action dans différents contextes éducatifs.

entry.title: Unboxing with Concordia University Press: Illuminating Media

media:group.media:title: Unboxing with Concordia University Press: Illuminating Media
media:group.media:description:
Join us with Saelan Twerdy as we unbox Illuminating Media: Transmitting the Renaissance in England, 1400–1550.
Bridging medieval studies, media studies, design history, and communications, Illuminating Media highlights the surprising endurance of these visual strategies in contemporary culture, from the design of passports to the layout of currency. Written in crisp, engaging prose, the book offers a new way of seeing both medieval art and modern design, making a powerful case for the value of interdisciplinary scholarship.
This book is now available in print and open access at press.library.concordia.ca.

entry.title: Unboxing with Concordia University Press: Literary Life After Death

media:group.media:title: Unboxing with Concordia University Press: Literary Life After Death
media:group.media:description:
Join Ryan Van Huijstee for the unboxing of Literary Life After Death: Commemorations of Writers in English-Speaking Canada by Carole Gerson.
Edited by Gabriela Aceves Sepúlveda, Analys Álvarez Hernádez, and Zaira Zarza.
Commemorations of deceased writers can often take on a life of their own—from the stately and serious to the kitschy and commercial. In Literary Life After Death, Carole Gerson categorizes and analyzes the ways in which writers have been commemorated in Canada to show how tributes have changed over time and in response to evolving national and social concerns.
This book is now available in print and open access at press.library.concordia.ca.

entry.title: Accueillir les élèves nouvellement arrivés avec Nathalie Auger et Caroline Dault

media:group.media:title: Accueillir les élèves nouvellement arrivés avec Nathalie Auger et Caroline Dault
media:group.media:description:
Rejoignez-nous pour le deuxième événement de Pluri-Ed-J 2 : Plurilinguisme en action – perspectives, ressources et pratiques!
Nathalie Auger, U. de Montpellier Paul-Valéry, et Caroline Dault, U. De Moncton, se rassemble pour l’enregistrement en direct de l’ épisode du balado “Plurilinguisme en éducation”, accueillir les élèves nouvellement arrivés.
PLURI-ED-J-2 mets de l’avant des modèles issus de la recherche, des ressources didactiques et pédagogiques, des ateliers ainsi que des pistes concrètes pour mettre le plurilinguisme en action dans différents contextes éducatifs.

entry.title: Explore the Leonard & Bina Ellen Art Gallery Vault! #montreal #art #curator #canadianart #quebec

media:group.media:title: Explore the Leonard & Bina Ellen Art Gallery Vault! #montreal #art #curator #canadianart #quebec
media:group.media:description:
Julia Eilers Smith, Max Stern Curator of Research and Collection, and Nicole Burisch, Director, give us a closer look inside the vault of the Leonard & Bina Ellen Art Gallery!
From the recent episode in our Closer Look series, where 4th Space explores the spaces and projects across Concordia that you might not otherwise get a chance to see!
In case you missed it, you can explore the lab by clicking the link above 🔗

entry.title: Plurilinguisme en Éducation - Présentation avec Catherine Maynard

media:group.media:title: Plurilinguisme en Éducation - Présentation avec Catherine Maynard
media:group.media:description:
Rejoignez-nous pour la troisième présentation de Pluri-Ed-J 2 : Plurilinguisme en action – perspectives, ressources et pratiques!
• Diffuser des textes identitaires plurilingues dans le cadre d'une bibliothèque vivante: l'expérience de personnes immigrantes en processus d'alphabétisation.
PLURI-ED-J-2 mets de l’avant des modèles issus de la recherche, des ressources didactiques et pédagogiques, des ateliers ainsi que des pistes concrètes pour mettre le plurilinguisme en action dans différents contextes éducatifs.
